text: Examining the beautiful game in a global civilisation, where cultures collide on the soccer pitch. Soccer is played in almost every country in the world, and makes a whole host of people very, very happy and very, very sad. It started life as a pretty mundane sporting activity, in the late nineteenth century, but rapidly became a major sociocultural activity in societies across the world. As such, since the late nineteenth century, soccer and all its permutations, has been studied by scholars all over the world, as an indicator of a range of sociocultural phenomena, from the political, to the economic, to the religious, to media and aesthetic levels, etc. Soccer & Society, the first ever international journal of sociology of soccer (or football as it is known in the rest of the world) is a fully refereed, global, interdisciplinary, academic journal which explores the sociocultural development of the game on a global level through a sociologically framed analysis of the dynamics of the relationship between sport and society, and vice versa. This journal looks to bridge the space between sport and society, exploring the dynamics and relationships between the games and the world they are played in. It is truly international, and welcomes, in English, alternative and innovative academic empirical work from any scholar in the world.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
